panels.title: Requests/sec by status class
panels.description: rate() is only valid on a counter. These are *_total, monotonic, and they survive a reload() — so a pool rotation does not show up here as a fake spike or a reset.

panels.title: Active connections by protocol
panels.description: Gauges: current readings, not totals. A retiring worker's value is dropped rather than inherited, so these fall to what is really open after a reload() instead of stranding a phantom.

panels.title: Requests/sec per worker
panels.description: Same counter, split by the worker label. One flat line among busy ones is a wedged worker.

panels.title: Total requests served
panels.description: Monotonic across the whole process lifetime, including workers that have already exited. It must never go backwards — that is what a reload() used to do.

targets.expr: tas_requests_total{worker=""} or on() sum(tas_requests_total) unless on() tas_requests_total{worker=""}

panels.title: Requests in flight
panels.description: Gauge. Climbs under load, returns to zero when the server drains.
